config.text: Texas Basemap Generator﻿
Documentation

Using The Print Feature﻿

The Print tool allows you to export the map area as a high‑quality image or PDF. Most users will only need the Map‑Only 4:3 templates, which produce clean, borderless outputs containing only the map—no legend, title block, or layout elements, ideal for pasting into block pages. 

All map‑only templates are set up in a 4:3 aspect ratio, which keeps your exported images consistent and avoids stretched or cropped results.

Opening the Print Panel

  1. Click the Print button on the widget toolbar.
  2. The Print Panel will appear on your screen. You can click and drag the top header bar to move it around.

Understanding the Print Area Frame

When the Print Panel opens, a red dashed rectangle appears on the map.

This represents exactly what will be captured in your PDF.

What the frame does:


  1. Shows the print extent.
  2. Stays centered on the screen.
  3. Maintains the correct aspect ratio required for the PDF template.

Print Tab Functions

The Print tab is where you prepare and create your PDF.

If you enter a file name, your exported PDF will use it.

  1. If left blank, the default name from widget settings will be used.
  2. If you want a sheet number to appear inside the PDF’s title block, add it here.


Use the checkbox to show or hide the red print frame on the map.


  1. ✔️ Enabled → Red print frame visible
  2. ❌ Disabled → Nothing shown on the map

(Showing it is recommended before printing!)

﻿Generate PDF


Click Print to:


  1. Capture the map image inside the red frame
  2. Insert the image into the PDF template
  3. Add title block elements (including optional page number)
  4. Download the final PDF
  5. Automatically add the frame to the Batch Printing tab

Batch Printing Tab

The Batch Printing tab stores all saved print frames created during the session.


What each batch entry contains:

  1. Print name
  2. Date/time it was created
  3. Options for visibility & snapping movement


Toggle Outline Visibility

Each batch item lets you show or hide its print boundary on the map.


  1. ✔️ Checked → Red outline is visible
  2. ❌ Unchecked → Outline is hidden


Remove a Single Batch Item

  1. Click Remove under the item you want to delete.
  2. Click Clear All to remove every batch frame from the map and the list.

Snapping

Snapping (Optional)

Snapping lets you move the map view one print-frame distance in any direction.

This is perfect for creating tiled map sheets.

Steps:


Enable Snapping at the top of the Batch tab.

Choose which frame is the Anchor.

Use ← ↑ ↓ → buttons to move the map exactly one frame to the side.

Tips for Best Results

  1. Keep the area fully inside the red frame
  2. Only what’s inside the red rectangle appears in the PDF.
  3. If your map is too zoomed out, the content may appear small in the PDF.
  4. Save PDFs before closing the widget
  5. Batch entries are cleared when the widget unloads or the map refreshes.

Troubleshooting

  1. ❓ I don’t see the red frame
  2. ✔️ Make sure Show Print Area is checked.
  3. ❓ My PDF is blank or missing map content
  4. ✔️ Ensure layers are visible
  5. ✔️ Wait until the map finishes loading before pressing Print
  6. ✔️ Avoid switching tabs while printing
  7. ❓ Snap buttons are disabled
  8. ✔️ You must enable Snapping
  9. ✔️ You must select an Anchor item
User Guide
1) Getting Started

What you’ll see on load:


  1. A full‑screen map centered on Texas.
  2. A top black toolbar with a Search bar and a row of tool buttons (e.g., Measure, Layers, Basemap, Draw, Legend, Bookmarks, Print).
  3. Map controls on the left: Home (default extent), Zoom In/Out, and (optionally) Locate.
  4. A scalebar along the map edge.
  5. A small ? button in the lower right corner.
2) Navigation & Map Basics

2.1 Pan and zoom


  1. Mouse: Drag to pan. Use mouse wheel to zoom in/out.
  2. Trackpad: Two‑finger drag to pan, pinch to zoom.
  3. Keyboard:
  4. Arrow keys to pan
  5. + / – to zoom (or Shift +/Shift –)

2.2 Left Map Controls


Home (default extent)


  1. What it does: Returns the map to the default starting view of Texas.
  2. When to use: If you’ve zoomed far away or lost your place.


Zoom In / Zoom Out


  1. What it does: Incrementally zooms the map in or out.
  2. When to use: For precise zoom steps instead of the mouse wheel.
  3. Shortcut: + to zoom in, – to zoom out.
3) Top Toolbar

3.1 Search (Find address or place)


  1. What it does: Searches for addresses, places, or points of interest.
  2. How to use:
  3. Click in the search bar or press / to focus (if supported).
  4. Type an address, place name, or coordinates (e.g., 30.2672, -97.7431).
  5. Press Enter or click the Search icon.
  6. Select a result to zoom to it; a marker appears at that location.
  7. Tips:
  8. Use complete addresses for best results (e.g., 100 Congress Ave, Austin, TX).
  9. Many coordinate formats are supported—decimal degrees or DMS.

3.2 Measure


Measure distances and areas on the map and view results in common units.

  1. Tools included:
  2. Distance: Click to start a path, click to add vertices, double‑click to finish.
  3. Shows total length (e.g., miles, kilometers).
  4. Area: Click to start a polygon, click to add corners, double‑click to finish.
  5. Shows area (e.g., acres, square miles, square kilometers).
  6. Unit selection: Choose units from the dropdown within the widget.
  7. Editing: Hover over a vertex until it highlights to move it.
  8. Clear: Click the trash can icon to remove all measurements.

3.3 Layers


Turn layers on/off, change drawing order (if supported), and open sub‑layer details.

  1. Layer list: Each layer shows a title with a box icon, check or uncheck this to toggle visibility.
  2. Sub‑layers: Expand a layer to reveal groups or categories (e.g., ‘Boundaries and Parcels will contain parcels, county boundaries etc).

Best practices:

  1. Performance: Turn off layers you don’t need to keep the map snappy.

3.4 Basemaps


Choose the background map you want to work with.

  1. What it includes: Satellite imagery, streets, topographic, terrain, dark/light gray, and possibly a Texas‑specific basemap.
  2. How to use: Open the Basemap gallery and click a thumbnail to apply it instantly.
  3. Tips:
  4. Some basemaps like ‘Outline Map’ won’t include street labels and address points. If you wish to include those, navigate to the ‘layers’ panel and toggle on your desired

3.5 Draw


Create simple graphics on the map—points, lines, polygons, text—to annotate or plan.

  1. Available tools:
  2. Point: Drop markers.
  3. Line / Polyline: Sketch routes or linear features.
  4. Polygon / Freehand: Delineate areas of interest.
  5. Text labels: Add notes or callouts.
  6. Style options: Choose color, outline, fill, line width, and label font/size.
  7. Editing: Select a shape to move vertices, reposition, or change style.
  8. Delete: Select a drawing and press Delete (or use the widget’s trash icon).
  9. Export: If your workflow includes printing or screenshotting, drawings are included in the current map view.
  10. Tips:
  11. Use the ‘draw’ feature to mark the edge of your printed area to line up multiple prints.
  12. See the video in section 9 of the Using the Print Feature page for tips on using the draw feature to line up multiple points

3.6 Legend


View the symbology (colors, icons, line styles) for visible layers.

  1. Dynamic: The legend updates based on which layers are turned on.
  2. Scale‑dependent: Some items appear only when you’re zoomed in enough for those features to draw.

3.7 Bookmarks

Save named map views so you can return to the same location and zoom level.

  1. Add a bookmark: Click Add, give it a name (e.g., “Panhandle—Site A”), and save.
  2. Use a bookmark: Click the bookmark to zoom back to that exact view.
  3. Organize: Rename or delete bookmarks in the panel (if supported).
  4. Persistence: Bookmarks are saved in your browser for this app; clearing site data may remove them.

3.8 Printing

For Information on the print widget see the 'Using the Print Feature' page.
